relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Grief is given bodily boundaries within covenant identity.
Israel is called holy and treasured, not superior.
Food becomes one way of practicing distinction.
Domestic herd animals are named as permitted.
Wild grazing animals are also listed.
Split hoof and cud become the land-animal signs.
Partial resemblance does not erase the dietary boundary.
Pig remains outside Israel's permitted food pattern.
Fins and scales mark permitted water creatures.
Water creatures without them remain outside the diet.
Clean birds may be eaten.
The prohibited bird list begins with great raptors.
Kites and falcons continue the list.
Ravens are excluded by kind.
Ostrich, owl, gull, and hawk are named.
More night birds enter the list.
Water and carrion birds are named.
Stork, heron, hoopoe, and bat close the list.
Winged swarming creatures remain outside the permitted pattern.
Clean winged life may be eaten.
Carrion law, foreign food economy, and the kid-in-milk boundary meet in one verse.
The field's increase is tithed year by year.
The tithe becomes a meal before YHWH that teaches reverence.
Distance from the sanctuary is treated as a practical problem, not a spiritual failure.
The produce may be converted into portable silver.
The silver becomes food, drink, desire, and household joy before YHWH.
The Levite must not disappear from the celebration.
Every third year the tithe stays local.
Levite, immigrant, orphan, and widow eat until satisfied.

contemplative_note: Deuteronomy 14 joins disciplined eating to abundant sharing. Holiness is not disgust; its mature fruit is a table where the landless and vulnerable eat enough.
